akragas.name: Akragas
akragas.introduction: Akragas is A fertile export city whose grain and food stores feed larger powers. on the sicily ionian. Its markets are known for grain, wine, olive oil, and the city survives through a mixture of regional alliances, harbor politics, and maritime trade. Sailors describe the port as pragmatic, watchful, and deeply tied to the fortunes of the sea.

aleria.name: Alalia/Aleria
aleria.introduction: Alalia/Aleria is A smaller but resilient harbor town dependent on sailors, fishing, and coastal exchange. on the tyrrhenian. Its markets are known for grain, and the city survives through a mixture of regional alliances, harbor politics, and maritime trade. Sailors describe the port as pragmatic, watchful, and deeply tied to the fortunes of the sea.

alexandria.name: Alexandria
alexandria.introduction: A colossal experiment at the mouth of the Nile. Scholars, grain magnates, sailors, and royal agents mix in a city that feels both Greek and Egyptian. Wealth flows like the Nile itself, but so do intrigue and imperial ambition.

antipolis.name: Antipolis
antipolis.introduction: Antipolis is A smaller but resilient harbor town dependent on sailors, fishing, and coastal exchange. on the iberia gaul. Its markets are known for grain, wine, and the city survives through a mixture of regional alliances, harbor politics, and maritime trade. athens_piraeus.name: Athens / Piraeus
athens_piraeus.introduction: Philosophers argue in Athens while hard coin changes hands in Piraeus. Shipyards, silver merchants, and debate halls create a city where prestige and commerce constantly reinforce one another.

byblos.name: Byblos
byblos.introduction: Byblos is A respected regional center balancing local production with maritime commerce. on the levant cyprus. Its markets are known for grain, wine, fish, and the city survives through a mixture of regional alliances, harbor politics, and maritime trade. Sailors describe the port as pragmatic, watchful, and deeply tied to the fortunes of the sea.

byzantion.name: Byzantion
byzantion.introduction: The narrow gate between the Mediterranean and the Black Sea. Whoever influences Byzantion can shape grain flows, tolls, and naval movement across entire regions.

caere.name: Caere
caere.introduction: Caere is A respected regional center balancing local production with maritime commerce. on the tyrrhenian. carthage.name: Carthage
carthage.introduction: Queen of western trade routes and the beating heart of Phoenician maritime power. Carthage is wealthy, disciplined, and ruthless in commerce. Grain fleets, silver, African estates, and long-distance spice caravans all converge here beneath ambitious merchant dynasties and wary admirals.

carthago_nova.name: Carthago Nova

Its markets are known for grain, wine, fish, and the city survives through a mixture of regional alliances, harbor politics, and maritime trade. Sailors describe the port as pragmatic, watchful, and deeply tied to the fortunes of the sea.

corinth.introduction: Built upon crossroads and ambition, Corinth profits from every ship and caravan passing between mainland Greece and the wider sea. Luxury goods and political maneuvering thrive equally here.

Its markets are known for grain, wine, and the city survives through a mixture of regional alliances, harbor politics, and maritime trade. Sailors describe the port as pragmatic, watchful, and deeply tied to the fortunes of the sea.
